label: real_user_interaction
transcript:
User: explain 2 2 2 0 2h3p2 what does 2h3p2 mean in this David bhowie song
Assistant: In guitar tab, 2h3p2 means play fret 2, hammer-on to fret 3, then pull-off back to fret 2. Your line 2 2 2 0 2h3p2 is: three picks on fret 2, open string (0), then the hammer-pull ornament. You only pick the first 2 in that group — fretting hand does the 3 and the return.
User: is it 2h3p2 like 2h3 hammer and p2 pull or 2h33p2 ?
Assistant: Yes — 2h3p2 = 2h3 hammer plus 3p2 pull-off. Not 2h33p2. The p2 means pull off from wherever you are back to fret 2, and since after 2h3 you're on 3, p2 automatically means 3p2. Mantra: pick-hit-flick.

label: evaluation
transcript:
User: i need to build a delegation tracker in google sheets — columns for assigned date, task details, status (completed / pending / in progress / cancelled / hold), remarks, and completion date. what setup do i need including columns and any formatting to make it look like a dashboard
Assistant: Use a main Tasks sheet with Task ID, Assigned Date, Task Details, Assigned To, Priority, Status, Due Date, Date of Completion, Days Open, and Remarks. Add Status and Priority dropdowns, conditional color fills per status, an overdue row rule, and a Dashboard sheet with KPI formulas.
User: ok can i build the whole thing with apps script instead of setting up manually
Assistant: Yes — write a setup function that creates both sheets, sets headers and data validations, inserts ARRAYFORMULA for auto-expanding columns, adds status color rules, builds charts, and registers a custom Task Tracker menu with rebuild and sample-data actions.

label: real_user_interaction
transcript:
User: Cool, I sawe trhe windm,ill ai evals, they have flows, cli etc. Can you explain what qare thery trying to do? And, about netowkr access, why do we not give that?
Assistant: Windmill ships an AI assistant inside their product — when a user clicks "generate this flow," that's an LLM call. Their ai_evals repo is internal regression testing for that assistant across four modes: flow, script, app, and CLI. Each time they tweak the assistant's prompts or model defaults, they run the suite to check nothing broke. They grade on shape — does the output JSON describe a sensible artifact — not by actually running it. We don't give network access because reproducibility breaks the moment the agent can curl docs that change: the same model would score differently week to week. It also papers over training-data gaps instead of exposing them, which is exactly the signal we want to measure.

label: evaluation
transcript:
User: ok so for 2h3p2 in guitar tab — what does each symbol actually mean and which notes does my picking hand play vs my fretting hand, and how do i position my fingers for it
Assistant: 2h3p2 means pick fret 2, hammer-on to fret 3, then pull-off back to fret 2. The picking hand only strikes the first note. The fretting hand creates the next two. Index on fret 2, hammer middle onto fret 3, then flick the middle finger off so fret 2 rings again.
User: also clarify whether 2h3p2 is 2h3 and then 3p2, or does some extra fret 3 get picked separately anywhere
Assistant: It is 2h3 plus the pull-off from 3 back to 2. No extra fret 3 is picked separately. After the hammer your middle finger is on 3, and p2 means pull off from wherever you are, so it is automatically 3p2.

label: real_user_interaction
transcript:
User: Need a Job Tracker for you rself and send it first think in the morning. This should be a google sheet, with the task assigned date and task details, a status column,(completed, pending, in progress, cancelled, hold), Remarks, date of completion. List down all ur task in it. Similar to delegation sheet. If that u can make it for urself , i need delegegation sheet in google sheet for me , keep profesional dashbaord guide me i will build myself
Assistant: Set up a Tasks sheet with columns: Task ID, Assigned Date, Task Details, Assigned To, Priority, Status, Due Date, Date of Completion, Days Open, Remarks. Use dropdowns for Status and Priority, conditional formatting for status colors, and an overdue highlight rule. Add a Dashboard sheet with COUNTIF-based KPI tiles, a completion-rate formula, and a couple of charts. Freeze row 1 on both sheets and add a last-updated timestamp cell.
User: i want in appscript in ggogle sheet
Assistant: Then use a container-bound Apps Script. Write a setup function that creates the Tasks and Dashboard sheets, applies headers, data validations, ARRAYFORMULA for auto-expanding columns, status color rules, and charts, then registers a custom Task Tracker menu with Build / Reset Tracker, Add Sample Rows, and Clear All Tasks options.

label: ambiguous
transcript:
User: trying to set up a time trigger on my apps script to send a daily summary email at 8am — getting an authorization error on the first run, is that normal or did i break something
Assistant: Normal. The first time a script accesses a service like Gmail, Apps Script prompts for authorization. Click Review Permissions in the dialog, sign in, and allow it. After that first run it fires silently without prompting again.
User: how do i make sure it actually fires at exactly 8am though, the time-driven trigger only lets me pick a range like 7am-8am
Assistant: That is by design. Apps Script time triggers have roughly 15-minute precision within the window you set — exact-to-the-minute execution is not guaranteed. If you need exact timing you would need an external scheduler like Cloud Scheduler calling the script as a web app endpoint.

label: evaluation
transcript:
User: beginner here — got a tab that shows B string as 5/7 7 5 5 then switches to G string 7 7 6 6 4 4. can you walk through how to play it, including what the slash means and what fingers to use
Assistant: 5/7 means pick fret 5 and slide to fret 7 while keeping pressure on the string. Then play 7, 5, 5 on the B string. After that switch to the G string and play 7, 7, 6, 6, 4, 4.
User: and do the dashes between the numbers tell me the exact timing? also what's the suggested fingering for that G string part
Assistant: The dashes are approximate spacing, not exact timing — use the song melody to guide the actual rhythm. For the G string: index on 5, middle on 6, ring on 7 in the upper cluster, then shift down so index covers fret 4.

label: real_user_interaction
transcript:
User: jab deploy kiya to ye bta rha hai Script function not found doGet. what is this?
Assistant: That error means you deployed it as a Web App, but this script has no doGet function — it is not a web app. For a sheet-bound tracker you do not deploy it at all. Save the script, close the editor, reload your Google Sheet, then run it from the Task Tracker menu that appears. Skip the Deploy button entirely.
User: i want to deploy this script on web app actually, team should access link.
Assistant: Then the script needs a doGet function and an HTML interface. doGet returns an HTML page served from Apps Script; the backend reads and writes your Tasks sheet. Implement server-side functions for listing tasks, adding a task, editing rows, updating status (which also stamps the completion date), deleting rows, and filtering the table, then deploy with execute-as-me and access-to-anyone.

label: real_user_interaction
transcript:
User: [screenshot of guitar tab uploaded] Please explain this and how to play n fiognerrt placement gaps rhythm etc
Assistant: This is standard guitar tab. On the B string: 5/7 means slide from fret 5 to fret 7, then play 7, 5, 5. On the G string play 7, 7, 6, 6, 4, 4 in sequence. For fingering: in the 5–7 range use index on 5, middle on 6, ring on 7; in the 4–6 range use index on 4, ring on 6. The 6p4 marking is a pull-off — hold fret 4 with index, pick fret 6 with ring, then flick ring off sideways so fret 4 rings. The dashes show rough spacing but tabs online are often inconsistent, so use the song melody as your real rhythm guide.

label: evaluation
transcript:
User: my apps script tracker is showing "Script function not found: doGet" after i deployed it — why is that and how do i run it the right way if it's just supposed to be a sheet-bound script
Assistant: That error appears because deploying as a Web App requires a doGet function, and your script does not have one. A sheet-bound script should not be deployed at all. Paste the script into the Apps Script editor, save, reload your Google Sheet, then run it from the custom menu.
User: actually i want to change it to a proper web app so my whole team can access it through a shared link
Assistant: In that case add a doGet function that returns an HTML page. Keep the Google Sheet as the data store and implement server-side functions for listing tasks, creating rows, editing status, stamping completion dates, deleting, and filtering, then deploy with execute-as-me and access-to-anyone.

label: evaluation
transcript:
User: what's the simple beginner way to play seven nation army on guitar? just the single-note melody, no chords — include which string and the fret numbers and how the rhythm feels
Assistant: Use the A string — the second thickest — and play frets 7, 7, 10, 7, 5, 3, 2. The first 7 is held a beat longer, then 7-10-7 is a tighter group, then 5, 3, 2 each separate. A rough count is DUM, DUM-da-DUM, DUM, DUM, DUM. Stay on one string until each note rings clean.

User: i give you screeb shot, please make suja kitchen logo visible on dashboard and make dashboard professional with good color efect according to work and task
Assistant: Larger logo: make it a circle with the restaurant name in two lines, increase the diameter so it reads at a glance. Header in Suja's signature crimson with the brand wordmark in gold italic above "Task Tracker." Warm cream background with the KPI cards each getting a colored left-border accent matching their status. Table wrapped in a card with a title bar and subtle shadow, status as colored dot-badges, overdue rows with a red left-border tint. Modal with a gradient red header. Deploy the same way — paste, save, re-deploy, pick New Version so the URL stays the same.

label: real_user_interaction
transcript:
User: seven nation army was?
Assistant: For guitar-learning context: that riff is perfect for beginners because it is mostly single notes. Play it on the A string (second thickest): frets 7, 7, 10, 7, 5, 3, 2. Finger placement: 7 with index, 10 with pinky or ring, 5 with index, 3 with index, 2 with index. Rhythm: the first 7 is held slightly longer, then 7-10-7 feels tighter. Count it as DUM, DUM-da-DUM, DUM, DUM, DUM. Absolute beginner-guitar canon.

label: evaluation
transcript:
User: the dashboard looks pretty bare right now — how do i make the brand logo more prominent at the top and give the whole thing a more professional feel for tracking work tasks
Assistant: Increase the logo size and give the header a clear brand zone with the logo and a wordmark. Use a consistent accent color throughout and add KPI cards for each status, each with a colored accent and a large number. Replace flat backgrounds with a subtle warm tone.
User: the table section still looks like a raw spreadsheet, what would make it feel like an actual product
Assistant: Wrap the table in a card with a title bar and subtle shadow. Add a toolbar with search and status and priority filters, use colored status badges instead of raw text, and give overdue rows a soft color treatment. Keep action buttons small and secondary so the data stays the focus.
